action.reasoning: Observation: Best AP=0.2191 (val~0.233) from iter27 with 4096-dim r2+r3 FP + 10 norm RDKit desc (4106 input), 2048/6-layer BN-MLP (drop=0.1); iter28 (7-layer/drop=0.05) regressed to 0.2112 test despite similar val due to overfitting (train loss~0.003, no recent jsonl curves, truncated train.py). PyG still segfaults. Analysis: Truncation prevents reliable runs; model/lr/patience/scheduler tweaks (layers=6/drop=0.1/lr=1e-4/patience=25/200 epochs/weight_decay=1e-5/clip=1.0/bs=4096/DataLoader) + full per-epoch metrics.jsonl logging (epoch/loss/val_ap/lr) + robust masked BCE (is_labeled mean) + filtered AP (valid tasks >5 samples, >1 class) will improve generalization on imbalance without cache invalidation or new deps. Time per run ~20min safe for remaining 1.6h/2 iters. Decision: Update model.py defaults to best arch, fully rewrite complete non-truncated train.py (with all fixes/logging/seed/DataLoader) and predict.py (match arch), run training, submit_eval on best.pt to exceed 0.2191.
